meaning: n.鏃朵唬绮剧,鏃朵唬鎬濇疆
examples: He has caught the zeitgeist of life in the 1960s very well indeed.
This time, too, Sir Jim has captured the Zeitgeist.
It is good at one thing, and for now the zeitgeist has turned against it.
It also plays into the cynical zeitgeist which supports the gravitational pull of the status quo.
Why do some celebrities, athletes and musicians seem to effortlessly shape the zeitgeist, while others--sorry, Hasselhoff--don't?
